summary: Closed, unmerged progress-report PR with no changed files or diff in the slice. The PR body reports an automated matching run: 5 functions matched, 7 failed, and 1 pending. All reported 100% matches are Kirby SpecialN animation functions in ftKb_SpecialN.c; failures span stage, player, UI, and unknown functions. Evidence is limited to the PR text because the slice contains no file changes, reviews, or diff.

The PR text recorded matching progress: 5 matched, 7 failed, and 1 in progress.
Reported matched functions were ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirNSpit_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialNDrink_Anim in ftKb_SpecialN.c.

smart_moves: The PR body separated automated matching outcomes into matched, failed, and in-progress buckets, making it easy to triage which symbols are ready versus which need more manual work.
Including best-match percentages for failed functions helps prioritize near-matches such as fn_8021E994 at 97.4%, grOnett_801E40E4 at 96.1%, and grCastle_801CE19C at 91.8%.
Grouping all successful matches in ftKb_SpecialN.c suggests focusing related Kirby neutral-special animation functions together can be an effective matching unit.
decomp_lessons: Progress-only PRs can still be useful as matching telemetry, but without a diff they should not be treated as evidence of landed code.
Near-match percentages are valuable for future decomp work: functions above roughly 90% may be good candidates for targeted cleanup, control-flow adjustment, or local variable/layout investigation.
When multiple related animation callbacks in one fighter-special file match together, reuse the successful style and structure for adjacent callbacks in the same subsystem.
Keep automated match reports searchable by exact symbol names, file names, and percentages so later agents can recover promising unfinished targets.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions use the ftKb_ prefix and CamelCase-like action names, as in ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Anim and ftKb_SpecialAirNSpit_Anim.
Air variants are named with SpecialAirN while grounded variants use SpecialN.
Unresolved or address-based symbols remain in forms such as un_80307828, fn_8021E994, grPura_80212FC0, grOnett_801E5194, grCastle_801CE19C, and pl_80038628.
Stage-related unresolved names in the report use gr prefixes, including grPura, grOnett, and grCastle.
The UI-related name InitNameEntryUIState appears as a descriptive named function rather than an address-style placeholder.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use the reported 100% Kirby matches as reference patterns for nearby ftKb_SpecialN.c animation callbacks.
Prioritize high-percentage failures for manual intervention: fn_8021E994 at 97.4%, grOnett_801E40E4 at 96.1%, and grCastle_801CE19C at 91.8%.
Lower-percentage failures such as InitNameEntryUIState at 37.3% and pl_80038628 at 55.6% likely need more structural recovery before fine-grained matching tactics are useful.
A function marked failed multiple times, grPura_80212FC0 failed 3x, should be treated as needing a different hypothesis rather than repeated identical automated attempts.
